title: Abx - Odontogenic
description: Antibiotic regimens for odontogenic infections — outpatient and inpatient options
bodyTemplate:
Antibiotic Therapy (Odontogenic Infection):

Outpatient — no penicillin allergy:
Amoxicillin 500 mg PO TID x 7 days
OR: Amoxicillin/Clavulanate (Augmentin) 875/125 mg PO BID x 7 days

Outpatient — penicillin allergy:
Clindamycin 300 mg PO QID x 7 days
OR: Azithromycin 500 mg PO day 1, then 250 mg PO daily x 4 days

Inpatient — no penicillin allergy:
Ampicillin/Sulbactam (Unasyn) 3 g IV Q6H
OR: Penicillin G 2-4 million units IV Q4H + Metronidazole 500 mg IV Q8H

Inpatient — penicillin allergy:
Clindamycin 600-900 mg IV Q8H
OR: Levofloxacin 750 mg IV daily + Metronidazole 500 mg IV Q8H

Severe/Ludwig angina:
Unasyn 3 g IV Q6H + Metronidazole 500 mg IV Q8H
Consider Dexamethasone 10 mg IV Q8H x 3 doses for airway edema
Consider Infectious Disease consult

Chlorhexidine (Peridex) 0.12% oral rinse — 15 mL swish and spit BID

title: Abx - Trauma
description: Antibiotic prophylaxis regimens for facial trauma
bodyTemplate:
Antibiotic Prophylaxis (Trauma):

Mandible fracture — no penicillin allergy:
Pre-op: Cefazolin (Ancef) 2 g IV
Post-op: Amoxicillin 500 mg PO TID x 5-7 days OR Augmentin 875/125 mg PO BID x 5-7 days

Mandible fracture — penicillin allergy:
Pre-op: Clindamycin 600 mg IV
Post-op: Clindamycin 300 mg PO QID x 5-7 days

Midface fracture:
Pre-op: Cefazolin (Ancef) 2 g IV if operative
Outpatient: no routine antibiotics unless open fracture or sinus involvement
If sinus involvement: Augmentin 875/125 mg PO BID x 5-7 days

Open wound / contaminated:
Unasyn 3 g IV Q6H; transition to Augmentin 875/125 mg PO BID x 5-7 days at discharge
Tetanus prophylaxis if not up to date

Through-and-through laceration:
Augmentin 875/125 mg PO BID x 7 days (OR Unasyn 3 g IV Q6H if inpatient)
Peridex 0.12% rinse BID for intraoral component

title: Consent - Anesthesia
description: Informed consent language for IV sedation and general anesthesia
bodyTemplate:
- Risks, benefits, and alternatives of consent-anesthesia:enumText:0 discussed with patient, including but not limited to: respiratory depression, airway obstruction, laryngospasm, bronchospasm, aspiration, allergic reaction or anaphylaxis to anesthetic agents, nausea and vomiting, cardiovascular depression or arrhythmia, prolonged sedation or delayed recovery, awareness during sedation, emergence delirium, damage to teeth or oral structures during airway management, sore throat or hoarseness, phlebitis or nerve injury at IV site, malignant hyperthermia, and — in rare cases — stroke, cardiac arrest, or death. Patient informed that an empty stomach is required to reduce aspiration risk.
- Alternatives discussed including treatment under local anesthesia alone, nitrous oxide sedation, oral anxiolysis, or referral to a hospital/ambulatory surgery center for general anesthesia.
- Post-operative restrictions reviewed: patient must not drive, operate heavy machinery, make legal or financial decisions, or consume alcohol for 24 hours following sedation. A responsible adult escort is required for discharge.
- Continuous monitoring (pulse oximetry, capnography, blood pressure, ECG) will be employed throughout the procedure. Emergency airway equipment and reversal agents are immediately available.
- Patient given the opportunity to ask questions and verbalized understanding. Written informed consent form signed by patient and witnessed.

title: Consent - Extraction
description: Informed consent language for dental extractions
bodyTemplate:
- Informed consent obtained. Discussed surgical indications, procedures, risks, benefits, and complications including but not limited to: pain, swelling, infection, bleeding, alveolitis, damage to adjacent teeth or restorations, fracture of the alveolar bone, maxillary tuberosity, or mandible, trismus or limited mouth opening, displacement of tooth or root fragment into adjacent anatomic spaces (maxillary sinus, infratemporal fossa, submandibular space), oroantral communication, and potential need for additional surgery; nerve injury resulting in altered sensation or numbness of the lower lip, chin, teeth, gingiva, tongue, or loss of taste — temporary or permanent. Discussed risk of periodontal pocketing and root sensitivity distal to mandibular second molars. Discussed possibility that a root tip or fragment may be retained if the risks of retrieval outweigh the benefits.

title: Consent - Implant
description: Informed consent language for dental implant placement
bodyTemplate:
- Informed consent obtained. Discussed surgical indications, procedures, risks, benefits, and complications including but not limited to: pain, swelling, infection, bleeding, damage to adjacent teeth or restorations, and nerve injury resulting in altered sensation or numbness of the lower lip, chin, teeth, gingiva, tongue, or loss of taste — temporary or permanent. Discussed risk of damage to adjacent tooth roots during osteotomy. Discussed risk of wound dehiscence or membrane exposure. Discussed risk of implant failure or loss of osseointegration requiring implant removal. Discussed risk of peri-implantitis, gingival recession, and bone loss around the implant. Discussed possibility of need for additional bone grafting, soft tissue grafting, or additional surgery. Discussed risk of damage to adjacent vital structures including the inferior alveolar nerve, mental nerve, lingual nerve, and maxillary sinus. Discussed risk of implant fracture, prosthetic component fracture, and need for future prosthetic maintenance or replacement. Discussed that implant treatment involves a multi-stage process with extended treatment timeline and that final restoration is not guaranteed.
- Alternative treatments discussed including no treatment, removable partial denture, and tooth-supported fixed partial denture. Risks, benefits, and limitations of each alternative were explained. Patient elects implant placement. Patient given the opportunity to ask questions and verbalized understanding.
- Written informed consent form signed by patient / legal guardian and witnessed.
- If sinus augmentation is planned: additional risks discussed including sinusitis, sinus membrane perforation, graft infection or loss, and oroantral communication.
- If IV sedation or general anesthesia is planned, anesthesia-specific risks were also discussed (see include:.sacxconsentanesthesia).

title: Exam - General
description: Standard OMFS physical exam for H&P and general consults
bodyTemplate:
Physical Exam:
General: Well-developed, well-nourished, no acute distress. Alert and oriented x4
Head: Normocephalic, atraumatic. No masses, lesions, or tenderness to palpation
Facial: No swelling, asymmetry, or ecchymosis
Eyes: No periorbital edema or ecchymosis. Extraocular movements intact without nystagmus. Pupils equal, round, reactive to light and accommodation. No injection, icterus, or foreign body
Ears: Atraumatic, no otorrhea
Nose: Atraumatic, no rhinorrhea
Oral: No lesions, ulcers, leukoplakia, erythroplakia, masses, purulence, or hemorrhage noted intraorally. Gingiva pink, stippled, well perfused, and non-edematous. Floor of mouth soft, non-elevated, non-indurated. Tongue mobile, midline, without masses or lesions. Hard and soft palate intact without clefting, tori, or ecchymosis. Uvula midline
Temporomandibular joint: Maximum incisal opening exam-general:blank:0 mm. Full range of motion without crepitus, clicking, or pain. No deviation or deflection on opening. Bilateral condylar translation palpable and symmetric
Dentition: exam-general:enumText:0
Occlusion: exam-general:enumText:1
Neck: Soft, supple, non-tender. Thyromental distance >3 fingerbreadths. Trachea midline. No subcutaneous emphysema
Lymph nodes: Bilateral submandibular, submental, jugulodigastric, and posterior cervical chains palpated — no lymphadenopathy
Heart: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs, rubs, or gallops. Peripheral pulses full and symmetric
Lungs: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. Chest expands symmetrically. No increased work of breathing
Neuro:
CN V: Sensation intact to light touch in V1 (ophthalmic), V2 (maxillary), and V3 (mandibular) divisions bilaterally. Masticatory muscles symmetric in bulk and strength without tenderness
CN VII: Symmetric forehead wrinkling, eye closure, smile, and lower lip depression bilaterally. No facial asymmetry at rest or with animation
CN XII: Tongue protrusion midline without fasciculations or deviation

title: Pain - Inpatient
description: Inpatient multimodal pain management regimen
bodyTemplate:
Pain Management (Inpatient — Multimodal):

Standard:
Ketorolac (Toradol) 15-30 mg IV Q6H x 72 hours (then transition to ibuprofen 600 mg PO Q6H)
Acetaminophen 1000 mg PO/IV Q8H scheduled
Morphine 2-4 mg IV Q3H PRN breakthrough
Ice packs to face 20 min on / 20 min off while awake

NSAID contraindicated:
Acetaminophen 1000 mg PO/IV Q8H scheduled
Morphine 2-4 mg IV Q3H PRN breakthrough OR Hydromorphone 0.5-1 mg IV Q3H PRN
Gabapentin 300 mg PO TID (if neuropathic component)
Ice packs to face

Severe / major post-op:
Ketorolac 30 mg IV Q6H x 72 hours
Acetaminophen 1000 mg IV Q8H scheduled
Morphine 2-4 mg IV Q3H PRN moderate pain
Hydromorphone 0.5-1 mg IV Q3H PRN severe breakthrough
Consider PCA if poorly controlled
Ondansetron (Zofran) 4 mg IV Q6H PRN nausea
Ice packs to face

title: Pain - Outpatient
description: Outpatient multimodal pain management regimen
bodyTemplate:
Pain Management (Outpatient — Multimodal):

Standard (no narcotics):
Ibuprofen 600 mg PO Q6H PRN pain x 5-7 days (take with food)
Acetaminophen 1000 mg PO Q8H PRN pain x 5-7 days
Alternate ibuprofen and acetaminophen every 3-4 hours for consistent coverage
Ice packs to face 20 min on / 20 min off while awake x 48 hours

Standard + tramadol:
Ibuprofen 600 mg PO Q6H PRN pain x 5-7 days (take with food)
Acetaminophen 1000 mg PO Q8H PRN pain x 5-7 days
Tramadol 50 mg PO Q6H PRN breakthrough pain x 3 days (#12, 0 refills)
Ice packs to face 20 min on / 20 min off x 48 hours

Standard + hydrocodone (short course):
Ibuprofen 600 mg PO Q6H PRN pain (take with food)
Acetaminophen/Hydrocodone 325/5 mg (Norco) 1-2 tabs PO Q4-6H PRN severe pain x 3 days (#16, 0 refills)
NOTE: Do not take additional acetaminophen while taking Norco — max 3000 mg/day from all sources
Ice packs to face 20 min on / 20 min off x 48 hours

NSAID allergy/contraindication:
Acetaminophen 1000 mg PO Q8H PRN pain x 5-7 days
Tramadol 50 mg PO Q6H PRN breakthrough pain x 3 days (#12, 0 refills)
Ice packs to face 20 min on / 20 min off x 48 hours

title: Post-Op Instructions - Extraction
description: Post-operative instructions handout — tooth extraction
bodyTemplate:
POST-OPERATIVE INSTRUCTIONS — TOOTH EXTRACTION

BLEEDING: Bite on gauze firmly for 30-45 minutes. Some oozing is normal for 24-48 hours. For persistent bleeding, bite on a moistened black tea bag for 30 minutes. Avoid spitting, sucking through straws, or smoking for at least 72 hours.

SWELLING: Apply ice packs 20 minutes on, 20 minutes off for the first 48 hours. Swelling typically peaks at 48-72 hours and resolves over 5-7 days.

DIET: Soft diet for 5-7 days (yogurt, smoothies without straws, mashed potatoes, scrambled eggs, soup, pasta). Avoid hard, crunchy, spicy, or acidic foods. Stay well hydrated.

ORAL HYGIENE: Do not rinse, spit, or use mouthwash for the first 24 hours. After 24 hours, rinse gently with warm salt water (1/2 tsp salt in 8 oz water) after meals. Brush other teeth normally — avoid the surgical site. Begin Peridex if prescribed.

MEDICATIONS: Take all medications as directed. Take pain medications with food. Do not exceed 3000 mg acetaminophen per day from all sources.

ACTIVITY: Rest the day of surgery. No vigorous exercise, heavy lifting, or bending for 48-72 hours. Keep head elevated.

SMOKING: No smoking or tobacco for at least 72 hours (preferably 1 week). Dramatically increases dry socket risk.

DRY SOCKET: Worsening pain 3-5 days post-extraction with foul taste may indicate alveolar osteitis. Contact our clinic.

FOLLOW-UP: post-op-instructions-extraction:blank:0

EMERGENCY: Uncontrolled bleeding, difficulty breathing or swallowing, fever greater than 101.5°F, rapidly worsening swelling — go to the nearest ED or call our clinic.

title: Post-Op Instructions - MMF
description: Patient instructions for maxillomandibular fixation (wired shut)
bodyTemplate:
INSTRUCTIONS FOR MAXILLOMANDIBULAR FIXATION (WIRED SHUT)

SAFETY:
Wire cutters have been provided. CARRY THEM AT ALL TIMES.
If you vomit or cannot breathe: CUT THE WIRES IMMEDIATELY to open your airway, then call 911.
Family members should know where the wire cutters are and how to use them.

DIET (LIQUID ONLY):
All nutrition must be liquid. Use a blender for everything. Drink through gaps between teeth or use a 60 mL catheter-tip syringe. Good options: smoothies, blended soups, milkshakes, Ensure/Boost, broth, juice, yogurt drinks, pureed meals. Aim for 2000+ calories/day and 60-80g protein/day. Stay hydrated: 2-3 liters of fluid daily.

ORAL HYGIENE:
Rinse after every meal. Peridex BID — swish gently, let flow between teeth. Use curved-tip syringe with warm salt water to flush between teeth and around wires after every meal. Use child-sized soft toothbrush on outer surfaces. Water pick on lowest setting optional.

MEDICATIONS: All medications must be in liquid or crushable form. Do not take whole tablets or capsules.

FOLLOW-UP: Weekly for first 2 weeks, then every 2 weeks. Anticipated duration: post-op-instructions-mmf:enumText:0. After removal: progressive mouth opening exercises, gradual diet advancement over 2-4 weeks.

EMERGENCY: Vomiting/choking (CUT WIRES FIRST), difficulty breathing, loose/broken wires, fever greater than 101.5°F, worsening swelling, foul drainage.

Rinse after every meal. Peridex BID — swish gently, let flow between teeth. Use curved-tip syringe with warm salt water to flush between teeth and around wires after every meal. Use child-sized soft toothbrush on outer surfaces. Water pick on lowest setting optional.

MEDICATIONS: All medications must be in liquid or crushable form. Do not take whole tablets or capsules.

FOLLOW-UP: Weekly for first 2 weeks, then every 2 weeks. Anticipated duration: ___ (4 / 6 / 8 weeks). After removal: progressive mouth opening exercises, gradual diet advancement over 2-4 weeks.

EMERGENCY: Vomiting/choking (CUT WIRES FIRST), difficulty breathing, loose/broken wires, fever greater than 101.5°F, worsening swelling, foul drainage.

title: Post-Op Instructions - ORIF
description: Post-operative instructions handout — jaw fracture repair (ORIF)
bodyTemplate:
POST-OPERATIVE INSTRUCTIONS — JAW FRACTURE REPAIR (ORIF)

SWELLING: Significant swelling is expected, peaking at 48-72 hours. Ice packs 20 min on/off for the first 48 hours. After 48 hours, switch to warm moist compresses.

DIET: post-op-instructions-orif:enumText:0. Ensure adequate protein and calories (goal 2000+ kcal/day). Supplement with Ensure/Boost if needed.

ORAL HYGIENE: Peridex (chlorhexidine) rinse BID starting POD1. Gently brush accessible teeth with a soft, child-sized toothbrush. Use a curved-tip syringe with warm salt water to irrigate surgical sites.

MEDICATIONS: Take all medications as directed. Complete the full course of antibiotics.

ACTIVITY: No contact sports, heavy lifting (greater than 15 lbs), or strenuous exercise for 6 weeks. No forceful nose blowing (especially midface fractures). Sneeze with mouth open.

NUMBNESS: Facial numbness is common, particularly lower lip/chin. Usually improves over weeks to months; may be permanent in some cases.

HARDWARE: Titanium plates and screws are MRI-compatible. Typically left permanently unless symptomatic.

ELASTICS: post-op-instructions-orif:enumText:1

FOLLOW-UP: 1 week, then 2, 4, and 6 weeks post-op.

EMERGENCY: Difficulty breathing, uncontrolled bleeding, inability to open airway (if MMF), fever greater than 101.5°F, vision changes, rapidly expanding swelling — go to the ED.

title: Proc - Coronectomy
description: Procedure block for mandibular third molar coronectomy
bodyTemplate:
MAND THIRD, CORONECTOMY
Attention was turned to site #proc-coronectomy:blank:0. A #15 blade and #9 periosteal elevator were used to develop and reflect a full-thickness mucoperiosteal flap with a distal release. A #8 round bur on a straight surgical handpiece with copious sterile saline irrigation was used to remove overlying bone and expose the tooth. The crown was sectioned and removed with a #703 fissure bur. The roots were left in situ and were reduced to approximately 2-3 mm below the level of the alveolar crest to allow bone formation over the retained roots. Complete removal of all enamel was confirmed with an intraoperative periapical radiograph. Clinical indication for coronectomy: intimate proximity of roots to the inferior alveolar nerve on pre-operative imaging, with unacceptable risk of nerve injury with complete extraction. No damage was noted to adjacent teeth or structures. All sharp bony edges were reduced. The site was curetted and irrigated with copious sterile saline solution (0.9%). The site was closed with 3-0 chromic gut suture.

IMPLANT WITH SINUS LIFT
Pre-operative residual bone height: proc-implant-with-sinus-lift:blank:0 mm
Attention was turned to edentulous site #proc-implant-with-sinus-lift:blank:1. Calipers were used to verify preoperative measurements and gingival thickness. A mid-crestal incision was made, favoring slightly lingual to preserve keratinized gingiva on the buccal aspect. Radiographs were taken periodically during the procedure with a parallel pin to confirm proper angulation and positioning. title: Proc - Mand Third Erupted
description: Procedure block for erupted mandibular third molar extraction
bodyTemplate:
MAND THIRD, ERUPTED
Attention was turned to site #proc-mand-third-erupted:blank:0. A #9 Molt periosteal elevator was used to reflect a conservative full-thickness mucoperiosteal flap to expose the tooth and interproximal bone. The tooth was elevated with dental elevators and delivered with 151 mandibular universal forceps. The inferior alveolar canal (IAC) was not visualized. No damage was noted to adjacent teeth or structures. All sharp bony edges were reduced with a rongeur. The site was curetted and irrigated with copious sterile saline solution (0.9%). The tooth was extracted intact with all roots accounted for. No sutures were placed.

title: Proc - Mand Third Impacted
description: Procedure block for impacted mandibular third molar extraction
bodyTemplate:
MAND THIRD, IMPACTED
Impaction: proc-mand-third-impacted:enumText:0
Attention was turned to site #proc-mand-third-impacted:blank:0. A #15 blade and #9 Molt periosteal elevator were used to develop and reflect a full-thickness mucoperiosteal flap with a distal release. A #8 round bur on a straight surgical handpiece with copious sterile saline irrigation was used to develop a buccal trough. The tooth was sectioned in a buccolingual direction using a #703 fissure bur, leaving the lingual portion intact. The sectioned portions were split and elevated atraumatically using straight and Cryer elevators. No damage was noted to adjacent teeth or structures. The inferior alveolar canal (IAC) was not visualized. Lingual nerve was not visualized. All sharp bony edges were reduced with a rongeur. All sectioned portions and roots were accounted for. The site was curetted and irrigated with copious sterile saline solution (0.9%). The site was closed loosely with 3-0 chromic gut suture.

title: Sinus Precautions
description: Sinus precautions patient instructions
bodyTemplate:
Sinus precautions:
- No nose blowing or forceful sniffing for sinus-precautions:blank:0 weeks.
- Sneeze or cough with the mouth open; do not pinch or occlude the nostrils.
- Avoid positive- or negative-pressure activities including spitting, sucking through straws, smoking or vaping, CPAP use, scuba diving, and playing wind or brass instruments until cleared.
- No flying for sinus-precautions:blank:1 days.
- Do not rinse vigorously or use oral irrigators (e.g., Waterpik).
- Limit bending at the waist, heavy lifting, and strenuous exercise to minimize sinus pressure changes.
- Elevate head with an extra pillow while sleeping for the first sinus-precautions:blank:2 days.
- If OTC decongestant is recommended: pseudoephedrine sinus-precautions:blank:3 mg PO qsinus-precautions:blank:4 h or oxymetazoline nasal spray BID x 3 days (do not exceed 3 days to avoid rebound congestion).
- If you notice air or fluid passing between your mouth and nose, or experience a salty or foul taste, contact the OMFS clinic immediately.
- Seek emergency care for: uncontrolled bleeding, progressive facial swelling with difficulty breathing or swallowing, or fever >101.5°F.
